metadata.label: gen2/Geico-SWE2
metadata.title: Software Engineer II
metadata.team_direction: GEICO is a leading insurance company that offers quality coverage to millions of customers

revision_priority: Trim the oversized Skills buckets so each category stays within 4-8 items and reads cleaner to ATS.
Add Code Review to Skills and make the backend ownership boundary in the transition story explicit.
把 `Code Review` 明确加入 `Skills`，让 JD 必须关键词在技能区完整出现。
portfolio_review.main_failure_reason: Trim the oversized Skills buckets so each category stays within 4-8 items and reads cleaner to ATS.

findings.issue: The opener frames the candidate as 'Transitioning from Analytics', so the first-screen signal is the transition narrative rather than the target-role identity.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to lead with the target role and core domain, e.g. 'Backend engineer with 3+ years building distributed services in Python/Go/Java...' and move the analytics-transition phrase later in the sentence.

findings.issue: This latest segment is dominated by tier-2 percentage lifts (28%, 19%, 31%) without a tier-1 scope anchor such as request volume, service footprint, or release cadence.
findings.fix: Add one concrete scope metric to the first bullet or project baseline, such as requests/day, number of services, daily analysts, or release frequency, so the impact reads as anchored rather than percentage-only.

findings.issue: The Temu section is almost entirely percentage/time-savings results, so the impact signal is weak relative to the role and lacks a scale anchor.
findings.fix: Add one scope metric such as pipeline volume, dataset size, report audience, or weekly query load to one bullet so the numbers become interpretable.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite Summary bullet 1 to lead with the backend role and distributed-systems domain, not the analytics-transition phrase.
[MUST] Add a concrete scope anchor to the TikTok intern section so the latest experience is not read as percentage-only impact.
[MUST] Add a concrete scope anchor to the Temu section so the earliest experience is not read as percentage-only impact.
[SHOULD] Keep the current technical coverage and two-project structure; the rest is broadly aligned.
raw_json.revision_instructions: [MUST] Rewrite Summary bullet 1 so it opens with 'Backend Engineer' and the core backend/distributed-systems stack; keep the analytics transition as a trailing clause, not the headline. [MUST] In the TikTok section, add one scope metric to the first bullet or project baseline (requests/day, services owned, analyst volume, or release cadence) so the latest experience has a Tier1 anchor. [MUST] In the Temu section, add one scope metric (pipeline volume, dataset size, report audience, or weekly query load) to one bullet to avoid an all-percentage impact pattern. [SHOULD] Leave the rest of the resume structure intact; the technical coverage is already strong.
